description: Glycolysis in *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* is a central metabolic pathway, converting glucose into pyruvate, ATP, and NADH. Its regulation is crucial for cellular energy homeostasis, biomass synthesis, and adaptation to changing environmental conditions. This flowchart details the intricate regulatory mechanisms governing glycolytic flux in yeast. Key control points include Hexokinase (Hxk), which phosphorylates glucose, and Phosphofructokinase-1 (Pfk-1), a major rate-limiting enzyme. Pfk-1 activity is finely tuned by the cell's energy status (ATP, ADP, AMP), allosteric activators like Fructose-2,6-bisphosphate (F2,6BP), and novel signaling molecules such as inositol pyrophosphates (IPPs), which act as inhibitors (Qin et al., 2023). Pyruvate Kinase (Pyk), the final enzyme, is also subject to allosteric control, notably by Fructose-1,6-bisphosphate (F1,6BP) and energy indicators.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A[Start Glycolysis] --> B[External Glucose]
    B --> C[Glucose Transport]
    C --> D[Intracellular Glucose]

    D --> E[Hexokinase (Hxk)]
    E --(consumes)--> F(\1)
    F --> G(\1)
    E --> H[Glucose-6-P (G6P)]
    H --(feedback inhibition)--> E

    H --> I[Fructose-6-P (F6P)]
    I --> J[Phosphofructokinase (Pfk-1)]
    J --(consumes)--> F
    F --> G
    J --> K[Fructose-1,6-BP (F1,6BP)]

    L[Low ATP/High AMP] --> R
    M[High ATP] --> T
    N[Fructose-2,6-BP (F2,6BP)] --> R
    O[High Inositol Pyrophosphates (IPPs)] --> T
    P[Low Inositol Pyrophosphates (IPPs)] --> R
    Q(\1) --> T

    R[Pfk-1 Activation?] --> S[Pfk-1 Activity Increased]
    S --> J
    T[/Pfk-1 Inhibition?/] --> U[Pfk-1 Activity Decreased]
    U --> J

    K --> V[Triose Phosphates]
    V --> W[Glyceraldehyde-3-P Dehydrogenase (Gpd)]
    W --(consumes)--> X(\1)
    W --> Y(\1)
    W --> Z[1,3-Bisphosphoglycerate]
    Z --> AA[ATP Production (Substrate-Level)]
    AA --> F
    AA --> AB[3-Phosphoglycerate to PEP]

    AB --> AC[Pyruvate Kinase (Pyk)]
    AC --(consumes)--> G
    AC --> F
    AC --> AD(\1)
    K --(feedforward activation)--> AC
    L --(activation)--> AC
    M --(inhibition)--> AC
    AE(\1) --(inhibition)--> AC
    AF(\1) --(inhibition)--> AC

    AG[Glycolytic Metabolon Formation] --> AH[Efficient Substrate Channeling]
    AH --> AI[Optimized Glycolytic Flux]
    AH --> AJ[Proximity to Vacuole/Mito]

    AK[Cellular Signals/Stress] --> AL[PTM (e.g., S-nitrosylation)]
    AL --> AM[Modulates Enzyme Activity]
    AM --(targets)--> E
    AM --(targets)--> J
    AM --(targets)--> AC

    AI --> AN[Sufficient ATP for V-ATPase]
    AN --> AO[V-ATPase Reassembly & Function]
    AO --> AP[Vacuolar Acidification]
    AQ[Pfk-1 Deficiency] --> AR[Reduced Glycolytic Flow]
    AR --> AS[Impaired V-ATPase]

    AD --> AT[Cellular Fates (Fermentation/Respiration)]
    F --> AU[Cellular Energy & Processes]
    Y --> AV[Redox Homeostasis]
    AI --> AW[Integrated Metabolic Output]
